action.reasoning: Observation: v19 reached promising 63 consecutive safe steps (min_d=1.12, detector=112) in ep0 but test crashed on NameError('self') from truncated/incomplete safe-count logic outside class; avg safe still low (~10-60), never hits 500. Config thresholds confirmed (500 accumulated steps in [1,2)m at |angle|<0.2618rad). Analysis: policy can find+follow briefly but drifts due to speed mismatch/oscillation/OA interference on dynamic oracle target; depth indexing fixed but test script broken. Root cause is incomplete files from prior truncation + follow gains not perfectly tuned for human velocity (~0.25m/s) and persistent facing. Literature heuristic can reach 0.5 follow-rate with tuning. Decision: rewrite COMPLETE non-truncated test_full_episode.py + predict.py with clean v20 HeuristicPolicy (detector>0.05 triggers follow at base_linear=0.25 + low err_gain=0.08 for speed-match, angular_k=8.0, pure-rotate if |angle|>0.4, OA disabled if detector>0.25 or only on min_front<1.1, backup<0.8m, refined depth valid>0.04 with center bias for min_front, manual per-step safe count + streak/max_safe tracking inside loop using policy attrs, full per-epi print of all diagnostics, use env.get_metrics() at end, make_serializable, abs paths + dataset override, proper action dict for both agents). Update train.py to v20. Run test_full_episode.py (5 eps) to validate improved streaks/safe_steps before submit_eval on predict.py (dummy ckpt). This should push some episodes >100 safe and increase chance of >=500 in lucky eps.
